visual: Side-stepping laterally while the hands trace continuous circles in front of the torso, one rising as the other falls, waist rotating smoothly.
motion_description: The practitioner executes a slow, continuous sequence of lateral sidesteps to the left, alternating between stepping out into a wide stance and drawing the trailing foot into a narrow stance. As the weight shifts to the left leg, the torso rotates roughly 45 degrees to the left, while the left arm sweeps across at face level and the right arm sweeps across at waist level. When the weight shifts back to the right leg to allow the left foot to step outward, the torso rotates to the right, and the arms reverse so the right hand is high and the left hand is low. Throughout the motion, the hands trace constant, alternating vertical circles in front of the body, rising and falling in coordination with the waist rotation.
